checks.description: Ensure that overlapping pulse patterns do not cause merged or spurious data_out assertions.
notes: Applies to edge case where input pattern contains back-to-back minimal pulses with a single-cycle gap.
checks.pass_condition: For each detected minimal pulse (0‑1‑0) the data_out signal must assert high for exactly one cycle and must return low before the next pulse detection; no consecutive high cycles are permitted when only a single-cycle gap exists between pulses.
checks.trigger_condition: When the edge_001 plan applies a pattern containing back‑to‑back minimal pulses (e.g., 01010).

checks.description: Validate that a single minimal pulse on data_in produces a single-cycle high on data_out exactly one clock after the pulse completes.
notes: Conservative timing: observe data_out on the clock edge following the third sample of the 0‑1‑0 pulse.
checks.pass_condition: When data_in follows a 0‑1‑0 pattern over three consecutive cycles, data_out must be low during the first two cycles and high on the third cycle, then return low on the subsequent cycle.
checks.trigger_condition: When the basic_001 plan drives the minimal pulse pattern on data_in.
